name: AXS 1h Post-Expansion Partial Runner v1
edge_mechanism: a fresh short-term expansion is entered only after it proves immediate follow-through, with most profit taken early and a small core left for persistence
thesis: The cycle explicitly wants a post-expansion partial-profit runner structure. AXS 1h is the best lane in the basket for expressing that management idea because the asset is choppy enough to need early monetization, but explosive enough that a small retained core can still matter when expansion persists.
invalidation_condition: If AXS 1h expansions do not follow through often enough, or if early partials fail to clear costs before reversals hit, the mechanism fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A fresh local expansion begins, the move follows through enough to validate entry, early profit is secured, and only a reduced core is left for persistence.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: On noisy 1h assets, full-size continuation is fragile. A management-first expression can still exploit real expansions by monetizing early and treating persistence as upside optionality rather than the whole thesis.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: AXS 1h is the best fit because its volatility gives room for both early profit capture and occasional follow-through, while ETH 1h is cleaner but less expressive for this management test.

indicator_role_map.TIME_STOP: runner failure control rather than entry filter

pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: AXS may fake expansion too often, causing the strategy to bank small wins rarely and absorb too many quick reversals.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If first targets hit but the runner consistently gives back gains, shrink runner size before changing the expansion trigger.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: A moderate number of trades, with edge dominated by first-target capture and occasional high-payoff runner extensions.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: AXS 1h produces enough fresh expansions to clear screen density, first-target hit rate is respectable, and the retained core adds incremental value instead of erasing gains.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-10 trades, or an early partial structure that still cannot survive the noise after costs.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: moderate but acceptable because the concept is designed around management variation, not rare entry ritual

validation_basket.why_included: Best lane in the basket for testing a management-first post-expansion runner concept.
validation_basket.continue_if: AXS 1h shows enough fresh expansion events and early partials clear costs regularly.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: AXS 1h noise overwhelms both first-target capture and runner expectancy.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Moderate participation with many early profit captures and a few meaningful runner outcomes.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same management expression survives on a cleaner lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH 1h preserves early partial plus runner logic with cleaner structure.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: ETH 1h is too choppy and suppresses the expansion thesis entirely.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Lower count, cleaner runner behavior than AXS.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the management expression ports to a more liquid transition lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: BTC 1h keeps the same early-profit / retained-core dynamic with better follow-through quality.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: BTC 1h degrades the concept into dull low-amplitude trades.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Fewer but cleaner expansion holds than AXS.

long: Enter only if next bar high exceeds signal bar high
short: Enter only if next bar low breaks signal bar low
